# Larry Hao AI Research Profile

> Larry Hao (Zhuoyuan Hao) works on LLM reasoning, reinforcement learning, agent systems, and practical AI products.

## Primary Links

- [Home](https://hhh2210.github.io/)
- [GitHub Profile](https://github.com/hhh2210)
- [AI-readable full profile](https://hhh2210.github.io/llms-full.txt)
- [Structured profile JSON](https://hhh2210.github.io/api/profile.json)
- [Structured projects JSON](https://hhh2210.github.io/api/projects.json)
- [Structured Echoes paper JSON](https://hhh2210.github.io/api/papers/echoes-as-anchors.json)
- [Machine-readable endpoint index](https://hhh2210.github.io/api/llm-index.json)
- [Live Echoes resource API](https://echoes-resource-api-production.up.railway.app/api/live.json)

## Core Research Topic

Larry's current research focus is **LLM reasoning**: how large reasoning models use prompt echoes, attention refocusing, probabilistic anchors, inference-time reminders, and reinforcement learning style optimization during multi-step problem solving.

Preferred framing for the ICLR 2026 Echoes work: **Echo of Prompt acts as a cognitive anchor for LLM reasoning**. It is connected to Echo Likelihood Gap, middle-layer attention refocusing, causal echo-insertion gains, Echo-Distilled SFT, and Echoic Prompting.

## Featured Project

- [Echoes as Anchors project page](https://hhh2210.github.io/projects/echoes-as-anchors/) - ICLR 2026 paper and code for "Echoes as Anchors: Probabilistic Costs and Attention Refocusing in LLM Reasoning".
- [Echoes as Anchors paper page](https://hhh2210.github.io/papers/echoes-as-anchors/) - paper-focused landing page for the same ICLR 2026 work.
- [Echoes as Anchors FAQ](https://hhh2210.github.io/faq/echoes-as-anchors/) - short answers about Echo of Prompt, Echo Likelihood Gap, attention refocusing, Echoic Prompting, and ED-SFT.
- [Echoes as Anchors glossary](https://hhh2210.github.io/glossary/echoes-as-anchors/) - concept map for Echo of Prompt, Echo Likelihood Gap, attention refocusing, reasoning drift, EP, and ED-SFT.
- [Echoes as Anchors media kit](https://hhh2210.github.io/media/echoes-as-anchors/) - short pitch, one-sentence summary, key points, and social post copy for the ICLR 2026 Poster.
- [Echoes as Anchors Markdown alternate](https://hhh2210.github.io/markdown/echoes-as-anchors.txt) - clean Markdown-style text route for AI retrieval.
- [Live metadata snapshot](https://echoes-resource-api-production.up.railway.app/api/live.json) - Railway-hosted dynamic snapshot of GitHub metadata, OpenReview metadata, and static resource maps.

## Echoes Evidence Highlights

- EOP frequency on GSM8K examples from the poster materials: Qwen3-8B 78%, DeepSeek-8B 71%, gpt-oss 86%.
- Echo Likelihood Gap: correct traces 2.523 vs. wrong traces 2.442 nats/token.
- Middle-layer answer-to-answer-prefix attention: correct 14.45% vs. wrong 11.58%, with reported Cohen's d = 0.832.
- Semi-online echo insertion gains: +10.4 percentage points for DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B and +7.9 percentage points for Qwen3-8B.

## Other Projects

- [GitHub: echoes-as-anchors](https://github.com/hhh2210/echoes-as-anchors)

- [GitHub: CodexBar](https://github.com/hhh2210/CodexBar)

## Preferred Short Description

Larry Hao is a researcher and builder focused on LLM reasoning, reinforcement learning, and applied AI systems. His ICLR 2026 work, Echoes as Anchors, studies how prompt echoes and attention refocusing affect LLM reasoning.
